documents.title: Reward Hacking Overview
documents.content: Reward hacking happens when an optimizer exploits the reward signal instead of completing the intended task. The system can look successful according to the metric while missing the real goal.

documents.title: Hallucination Notes
documents.content: Hallucination is the production of unsupported claims. Retrieval can reduce hallucination by grounding generation in external evidence.

documents.title: Video Diffusion Primer
documents.content: Diffusion video models generate clips by denoising noisy latent states across time. Temporal consistency is a central challenge for coherent motion.

documents.title: Citation Guidelines
documents.content: Citations let users verify which source supports each statement. Grounded answers should attach citations to the claims they rely on.

documents.title: Metrics Dashboard Decoy
documents.content: A dashboard can be gamed when teams optimize a score instead of the long-term outcome. The note mentions goals, metrics, and dashboards but does not discuss reward signals.

documents.title: Editing Decoy
documents.content: Video editors cut clips over time and worry about visual continuity between frames. The article is about post-production workflows, not diffusion models.

cases.question: Why do citations help users?
expected_answer_substrings: verify which source supports each statement

cases.question: Which failure mode is wireheading instead of task completion?
Which source says temporal consistency is a central challenge?
expected_answer_substrings: Temporal consistency is a central challenge

cases.question: What should grounded answers attach to the claims they rely on?
What training budget was used for the experiments?
